title: Floral Light Mexican Cardamom Stir-fry
description: A light and floral Mexican dish featuring ground cardamom in a Mediterranean-friendly preparation that's perfect for quick weeknight meals.
instructions: In a bowl, combine ground cardamom and olive oil. Mix well until evenly coated.
Place chicken pieces in the marinade and marinate for at least 30 minutes or overnight in the fridge.
Heat a bit of olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add onions and garlic, sautéing until they are translucent and fragrant.
Add bell peppers and jalapenos to the pan. Cook for another 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Drain the chicken from its marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off. Place on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake in the preheated oven for about 18-20 minutes or until the chicken is fully cooked and golden brown.
While the chicken is baking, chop tomatoes into small pieces. Sauté them gently with olive oil for 3-5 minutes, adding salt to taste.
Remove chicken from oven and let it cool slightly. Slice the avocado and add it to the stir-fry along with red onions.
Pour reserved marinade on top of the stir-fry mixture. Add lime juice and remaining olives, tossing gently until everything is well combined.
